title: Chevron Houston Marathon returns to downtown under ideal January conditions
content_md:
Runners gathered in downtown Houston on Sunday, January 19, 2025 for the Chevron Houston Marathon, one of the largest marathons in the United States and a qualifier for both the Boston Marathon and the U.S. Olympic Marathon Trials.

The race started and finished at the George R. Brown Convention Center, winding through downtown, Midtown, the Heights, and Memorial Park on a flat course that is popular with runners chasing personal-best times. Weather on race morning was cool and calm, conditions long considered ideal for distance running in the city.

Crowd support along Heights Boulevard and through Memorial is a trademark of the event, with front yards and neighborhood corners becoming impromptu aid stations and cheering sections. The companion Aramco Houston Half Marathon ran simultaneously on an abbreviated route.

After the finish, participants gathered in the convention center's post-race food hall for the hot broth, grilled cheese, and oversized medals that have become annual traditions at the Chevron-sponsored event.

title: 49th Bayou City Classic draws 3,000 to downtown Houston as RRCA State Championship
content_md:
The 49th running of the Bayou City Classic 10K and 5K Fun Run returned to Hermann Square adjacent to Houston City Hall on Saturday, March 7, 2026, drawing more than 3,000 runners, walkers, and volunteers to the streets of downtown Houston.

The Bayou City Classic, the oldest 10K in Texas, was selected as the 2026 RRCA State Championship 10K, adding an extra layer of competition to an already storied event. The 10K started at 8:00 AM with the 5K Fun Run/Walk following at 8:20 AM, both courses winding through downtown along Buffalo Bayou.

The flat, fast course that starts and finishes at Sam Houston Park has been a Houston tradition for more than 45 years, making it one of the city's most established running events. The race is organized by the Houston Area Road Runners Association (HARRA) and draws a mix of competitive runners chasing personal records and recreational joggers enjoying the downtown scenery.

Post-race festivities at Hermann Square included awards, food, and the community atmosphere that has kept runners coming back to the Bayou City Classic for nearly five decades.

title: The Woodlands Marathon weekend fills The Woodlands with distance runners
content_md:
The Woodlands Marathon weekend took place on Saturday, March 7, 2026, bringing marathon, half marathon, 10K, 5K, and 2K participants to the scenic streets and trails of The Woodlands, Texas.

The course winds through the master-planned community's tree-lined neighborhoods and along the waterway, offering one of the most spectator-friendly marathon courses in the Houston metro area. The event has grown steadily since its inception, attracting runners from across Texas and beyond who appreciate the flat terrain and well-organized aid stations.

Multiple distances made the weekend accessible to runners of all levels, from first-time 5K participants to experienced marathoners using the race as a final tune-up before spring ultra season. The 2K fun run gave families and young runners a chance to participate in the weekend's festivities.

Results were posted via MyChipTime, with full marathon, half marathon, 10K, and 5K standings available on the official race website. The Woodlands Marathon has established itself as one of the premier early-spring distance events in the greater Houston area.

title: H-Town Half Marathon sets course records at Minute Maid Park
content_md:
The H-Town Half Marathon, 10K & 5K presented by Houston Methodist took over the streets around Minute Maid Park on Saturday, March 14, 2026, with runners setting new course records across multiple distances.

Aaron Albert blazed the half marathon course in 1:11:49, setting a new men's course record. Christopher Wallace claimed the 5K men's course record in 15:59, while Virginia Wallace set the women's 5K course record at 18:31. The half marathon started at 7:40 AM with the 5K and 10K following at 8:00 AM.

The courses offered scenic views of downtown Houston, running entirely on paved streets and paved trails near the ballpark district. The half marathon carried a 3:30 cutoff and the 5K/10K a 1:30 cutoff, accommodating runners and walkers of all paces.

The H-Town race is part of the Run Houston Race Series presented by Houston Methodist, one of the city's premier race series that showcases unique venues throughout the metro area. Award ceremonies followed each race, with the 5K awards at 9:00 AM, 10K at 9:45 AM, and half marathon at 10:15 AM.

title: Yuri's Fun Run returns to Nassau Bay to celebrate space history
content_md:
Runners gathered in Nassau Bay on the morning of Saturday, April 4, 2026 for the annual Yuri's Fun Run Houston, a chip-timed 5K and 10K held at St. Thomas the Apostle Episcopal Church on Upper Bay Road in the Clear Lake community. The Kids 1K went off at 7:15 a.m., followed by the 5K and 10K at 7:35 a.m. Conditions were typical for early April in the Houston area.

The course wound through the streets of Nassau Bay, a small municipality where Apollo-era astronauts, engineers, and flight directors have lived since the early days of the space program. The neighborhood setting gives the race a distinct character among Houston-area road events, with residential streets leading past homes with a direct connection to American spaceflight history.

In the 10K, Virginia Frank crossed the line first among all finishers with a chip time of 40:57 — the fastest overall performance of the morning. Patrick Scott took the top men's spot in 44:13. In the 5K, Richard McEvoy-Kemp won the men's division in 19:20, and Charlotte Maples led the women's field in 21:37. The 5K drew more than 230 finishers across all age groups, with competitive fields stretching from children's divisions through the 85-89 age group.

The race is staged as part of Yuri's Night, a global celebration marking two milestones that both fell on April 12: Yuri Gagarin's first human spaceflight in 1961 and the inaugural launch of the U.S. Space Shuttle in 1981. The Houston edition is scheduled on or near that anniversary each year. Proceeds benefit the Houston Learning Center of the Challenger Center for Space Science Education. The event is part of the Texas 5K Series organized by Running Alliance Sport.

title: 18th annual green6.2 draws Earth Week crowd to CITYCENTRE
content_md:
Houston Running Co. hosted the 18th annual green6.2 at CITYCENTRE on Saturday, April 4, 2026, drawing runners to the mixed-use development at 800 Town and Country Boulevard in the Energy Corridor area for one of the spring season's most consistent local events. The 10K and 5K started at 7:30 a.m., with a kids and family 1K run following at 9:30 a.m.

The race is staged each year around Earth Week and built its identity on sustainability practices from the outset. The 2026 edition continued those commitments with digital race packets, reusable water stations, a medal recycling option, and a shoe drive at the finish line. Organizers encouraged participants to carpool or bike to the event. All participants received a finisher medal and technical t-shirt; age group awards were presented to the top finisher in ten-year brackets for both the 5K and 10K.

The event benefits three local nonprofit organizations, though specific beneficiaries for the 2026 edition were not listed in advance materials reviewed. The race began in 2008 with approximately 200 participants and has grown to more than 2,000 in recent years, making it one of the larger spring road races in Houston. It is produced by Houston Running Co., which also manages the Houston Running Co. Holiday Series.

A virtual race option was offered alongside the in-person distances for participants who could not attend on race day.

title: Third annual PEEPS race fills Energy Corridor on Easter weekend
content_md:
The third annual Running With My PEEPS 5K/10K took place on Saturday, April 4, 2026 at the Addicks Park and Ride on the Katy Freeway in Houston's Energy Corridor district, coinciding with Easter weekend. The Kids Dash started at 8:00 a.m., the 10K at 8:15 a.m., and the 5K at 8:20 a.m.

The course followed a flat, out-and-back route along Park Row Drive adjacent to the Addicks Park and Ride complex, a format the organizers have used since the event's inaugural edition. The race is part of the official PEEPS 5K/10K Run Series, a nationally licensed series tied to the Just Born candy brand. Participants received officially licensed PEEPS finisher medals, performance tech shirts, and participant bibs. Post-race refreshments included PEEPS marshmallow candy.

Festivities at the finish area included a PEEPS chick mascot appearance, an Easter Bunny visit, and a kids' Easter egg hunt station. The event registration was capped at 1,500 participants; prior editions sold out ahead of race day. The race also offered a virtual option for remote participants.

The event is produced independently and is not affiliated with a local running club or charity series, though it operates as part of the broader PEEPS-branded national run series.

title: Brazos Bend 50 sends runners past alligators on Texas's flattest trail course
content_md:
Trail Racing Over Texas hosted the spring edition of the Brazos Bend 50 at Brazos Bend State Park in Needville on Saturday, April 11, 2026, with distances ranging from 5K to 50 miles on one of the flattest trail courses in the state.

The 50-mile event started at 6:00 AM under headlamps, with the 50K following at 7:00 AM, the 25K at 7:30 AM, the 10K at 8:00 AM, and the 5K at 8:30 AM. The finish line remained open until 9:00 PM, giving ultrarunners a full 15 hours to complete the 50-mile distance.

The course traverses hard crushed rock, soft hiking trails under tree canopy, and horse trails through the park's 5,000 acres. Brazos Bend is famous for its resident alligator population, and runners frequently report spotting the reptiles sunning themselves along the levees that border the park's lakes. The course is 99.9% flat with only a single 10-foot rise, making it a popular choice for first-time ultra runners and those chasing fast times on dirt.

The 2026 50K served as the RRCA Texas State Championships, drawing competitive trail runners from across the state. Trail Racing Over Texas hosts multiple events at Brazos Bend throughout the year, and the post-race taco tradition at the finish-line tent continues to be a staple of the southeast Texas ultra running community.
